JavaScript tests for `Math.round` function with various inputs including `NaN`, zero, infinity, and decimals.


## Files
- **[15.08.02.15-001.js](15.08.02.15-001.js.md)**: Tests that `Math.round` returns `NaN` when the input is `NaN`.
- **[15.08.02.15-002.js](15.08.02.15-002.js.md)**: Tests the behavior of `Math.round` with positive zero in JavaScript.
- **[15.08.02.15-003.js](15.08.02.15-003.js.md)**: Tests the behavior of `Math.round` with negative zero in JavaScript.
- **[15.08.02.15-004.js](15.08.02.15-004.js.md)**: Tests the behavior of the Math.round function with Infinity as input.
- **[15.08.02.15-005.js](15.08.02.15-005.js.md)**: Tests the behavior of the Math.round function with negative infinity.
- **[15.08.02.15-006.js](15.08.02.15-006.js.md)**: Tests the behavior of `Math.round` function when dividing 1 by its result.
- **[15.08.02.15-007.js](15.08.02.15-007.js.md)**: Tests the behavior of `Math.round` with negative decimal input in JavaScript.
